pi_items.source: Web search and fetch - books.toscrape.com is a well-documented demo website for web scraping, explicitly designed as a sandbox for scraping practice
Verified in setup_task.sh (line 23-24) and export_result.sh (line 11) - this is the consistent output path configured in the task scripts
Web search found that a typical tree graph PNG can be ~4KB (256x256 pixels), so 5KB is a reasonable minimum threshold for a directory tree visualization

Web fetch from books.toscrape.com shows: (1) 'catalogue' appears in URL patterns like 'catalogue/category/books/...'; (2) 'books' is in the site title 'Books to Scrape' and throughout content; (3) 'toscrape' is in the domain 'toscrape.com' and site branding 'books.toscrape.com'

pi_items.metadata_value: Directory Tree Graph visualization in Screaming Frog
pi_items.verified_value: Directory Tree Graph visualization exists in Screaming Frog SEO Spider
pi_items.source: Web search of Screaming Frog documentation confirms 'Directory Tree Graph' is one of the available site visualizations alongside Force-Directed diagrams. Referenced in official Screaming Frog blog and user guide

privileged_info_summary: The task targets https://books.toscrape.com/, a real, well-documented public demo website specifically designed for web scraping practice. The website contains the expected content: 'catalogue' appears in URL navigation patterns (catalogue/category/books/...), 'books' is featured throughout the site title and product listings, and 'toscrape' is part of the domain (toscrape.com). The expected output path (/home/ga/Documents/SEO/reports/site_tree.png) is correctly configured across task scripts. The minimum file size threshold of 5KB is reasonable for PNG directory tree visualizations. Screaming Frog SEO Spider does include a 'Directory Tree Graph' visualization feature. The data used is real (from a live public website), not synthetic.
